page: Microsoft Azure L16s_v4 is a storage-optimized virtual machine from the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 3840 GB capacity that is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.49 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, it reached a top-tier text generation speed of 369.86 tokens per second. Other workloads, such as multi-core stress-ng (15996.5) and large-block memory write bandwidth (67.83 GB/sec), perform within the average range. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

Given its high local storage capacity of 3840 GB and 128.0 GB RAM, the L16s_v4 offers a resource-dense profile for data-intensive applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large local scratch space or high-throughput database operations without the overhead of external storage attachments. This server is well-suited for relational databases, NoSQL stores, data warehousing, and low-latency transactional applications. It also fits software compilation, text processing, and lightweight CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v4 is a storage-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 3840 GB Premium Storage capable local drive. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and a top-tier text generation speed of 369.86 tokens per second for small LLM models. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enhancing vector and matrix calculations. With its high local storage density and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is designed for database hosting, large-scale transactional workloads, and data-intensive applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v4 is a storage-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB of local storage, designed for high-throughput database and transactional workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v4 is an Intel-powered virtual machine with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, dense local storage, and advanced matrix extension support for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory paired with a bundled 3840 GB Premium Storage capable local drive.
Strong single-core performance with top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models.
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets.
Optimized for relational databases, NoSQL platforms, and high-throughput transactional applications.
